description_long: This temple features a raised wooden platform with a set of stairs leading to a covered porch supported by columns. The roof is steeply pitched and pyramidal, clad in dark shingles, and overhangs the main structure, providing shelter to the porch area. The entrance is flanked by two golden, tiered lamp-like structures, and the approach is marked by scattered stones on a flat, paved base. The walls are simple and unadorned, with visible wooden beams and open spaces beneath the roof eaves. The overall design is compact, with a symmetrical layout and minimal exterior ornamentation. The structure appears to be inspired by traditional South Indian or Kerala temple architecture, emphasizing wood and stone materials.
description: A compact wooden temple with a steep pyramidal roof, raised platform, and golden lamp structures at the entrance, set on a paved base with scattered stones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, stairs, porch, and two golden lamp structures flanking the approach.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open porch, supporting columns, and the profile of the steep roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, roof overhang, and the steps leading down from the platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the porch, stairs, and the arrangement of stones and lamp structures from a different angle.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist with golden tiered lamps and simple wooden beams
attribute_entrance_design: Straight staircase leading to a columned porch, flanked by two golden, tiered lamp structures, with a simple rectangular doorway and minimal ornamentation.
